title: End Of Evangelion Out Of Print
display_title: End Of Evangelion Out Of Print
wikitext:
[]: End Of Evangelion Out Of Print?             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jan 23, 2006 2:24 pm I went to town today to pick up my platinium editions. While there, I asked about ordering End Of Evangelion, only to be told it was out of print ? Does anyone have any information on this?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 23, 2006 2:36 pm Now bundled with Death & Rebirth in this package .

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Jan 23, 2006 7:18 pm You know, when we have topics like this, people should try to be a little clearer about what DVD region/country they're in. For instance, you two are both in England if I remember. That being said, I believe Manga is probably only making the boxset in the US as well. And the "mousepad" they include is completely worthless.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Mon Jan 23, 2006 7:23 pm What's on the mousepad? I want one even if it is worthless! Also how can a DVD be out of print....its not like you can read them!

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Jan 23, 2006 9:05 pm SEELE 08 wrote: What's on the mousepad? I want one even if it is worthless!

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Mon Jan 23, 2006 9:43 pm Oh damn I thought it was going to have all the characters in it or something. But anyways I don't think its useless, I think it is a laptop mousepad for the minimice that work with them.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Tue Jan 24, 2006 12:04 pm Yeah, Sachiel has the mousepad. It would look nicer framed them used for a mouse.

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Tue Jan 24, 2006 12:09 pm drinian wrote: Edit: Sure enough, here's a photo: http://images12.fotki.com/v210/photos/1/131573/2568603/P1010042-vi.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Jan 24, 2006 5:02 pm They must be pretty serious about pushing that D&R.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jan 25, 2006 1:27 am Whatever the case, Manga Entertainment survives by pushing their DVDs in great volume. They survive not by licensing and producing titles, but by getting every damn person to buy a copy of Ghost in the Shell and The End of Evangelion. Don't worry, I'm sure it's just a mistake. That or Manga has given us more ammunition to hate them.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Mon Feb 20, 2006 5:22 pm Usually if a title goes out of print on DVD it's because the publisher is planning on releasing a new edition. If the new addition is the bundle, then that blows, and so does Manga

[]:             Quote Post by BLACKANGEL32076 » Sat Jul 01, 2006 1:20 am With all the time that's gone by, what is the status of the twin pack?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 01, 2006 1:28 am As far as I'm aware, both the 2 DVD set and the individual DVDs are still available. I can tell you that Manga Entertainment renewed their license just recently, and there's some rumor of possibly seeing a remasted EoE release in conjunction with all the 10th Anniversary stuff floating around.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sat Jul 01, 2006 6:24 am The Eva Monkey wrote: As far as I'm aware, both the 2 DVD set and the individual DVDs are still available. I can tell you that Manga Entertainment renewed their license just recently, and there's some rumor of possibly seeing a remasted EoE release in conjunction with all the 10th Anniversary stuff floating around.
